_note: Industry feed: Space / EO / climate / maritime news in dry insider voice. Refreshed every ~3 weeks by GHA cron.

items.headline: Spanish EO startup Xoople raises $130M Series B to map Earth for AI, signs sensor contract with L3Harris
items.snark: Another promise of AI-ready constellations, this time with actual hardware orders attached instead of just slide decks.

items.headline: Vantor announces dual EO constellations: 20cm high-fidelity and 40cm rapid-revisit smallsats
items.snark: Even the resolution champion is accepting that the market wants both high-res and high-frequency, finally.

items.headline: EO operators push to deliver satellite images within minutes as government customers compress decision cycles
items.snark: Two years ago they wanted 90 minutes, now they want minutes, and nobody is asking whether faster always equals better decisions.

items.headline: Vantor partners with Windward for automated maritime tracking using high-resolution satellite imagery
items.snark: Product partnerships are the new distribution partnerships, at least when you can define the trigger and automate the payout.

items.headline: Starcloud raises $170M Series A at $1.1B valuation for space data centers, near-term revenue from EO edge computing
items.snark: They say orbital compute but the fine print says EO workloads are paying the rent, just like government contracts built the first constellations.

items.headline: Viasat confirms ViaSat-3 F3 launch for April 27 on Falcon Heavy to complete Asia-Pacific constellation
items.snark: Final bird in a constellation that was supposed to change everything before the first one failed, now just trying to catch up.

items.headline: Blue Origin launches third New Glenn with first booster reuse, but payload ends up in wrong orbit
items.snark: Recovery worked beautifully but the satellite is in the wrong place, which is the expensive part of the mission.

items.headline: Global Fishing Watch receives $60M to map one million vessels and all fixed ocean infrastructure using AI over five years
items.snark: The funding is real and the ambition is clear, but comprehensive coverage still depends on countries deciding to share VMS feeds.

items.headline: High seas treaty summit ends with dispute over fisheries managers' role in new marine protected areas
items.snark: RFMOs want consultation rights on MPAs they've spent decades not protecting, and conservationists are unimpressed with the power grab.

items.headline: 24 new deep-sea species found in Clarion-Clipperton Zone as U.S. streamlines seabed mining permits
items.snark: NOAA's new rule lets companies apply for exploration and recovery permits simultaneously, because discovering biodiversity shouldn't slow extraction.

items.headline: Inside Climate News: Next El Niño could lock Earth into hotter climate, pushing past 1.5°C threshold permanently
items.snark: Climate scientists watching the Pacific like it's a cauldron about to boil over, and the forecast is we don't cool back down afterward.

items.headline: Ghana declares first marine protected area covering 703 sq km at Cape Three Points spawning grounds
items.snark: A no-take core zone surrounded by regulated multiple-use areas, which is the design everyone wants and few governments actually enforce.
